Merged 1cf7f6ac; both facets demonstrated on the promoted operator against the original reproduction.

gaps.title: Gate satisfaction buckets the STORED status of derived-status kinds — currentState simultaneously derives a milestone reached and reports it as an unsatisfied gate
gaps.description: currentState's isCompleted (context-sdk.ts:787-790) buckets an item's STORED status for gate satisfaction (unsatisfiedGates, :835-836), while the SAME read derives milestone status from the phase rollup in memory (milestones[], :946-963) — two truth sources inside one payload. The milestone schema declares status 'DERIVED phase-rollup, not authored: … authored status is rejected by canon', yet nothing updates or guards the stored field, and the reached-milestone-phases-complete invariant guards only the stored-reached direction (when_bucket: complete), never stored-planned-with-all-phases-complete. Live instance: MILE-003 stored 'planned' while its phase completed — currentState reports MILE-003 reached in milestones[] AND lists it as an unsatisfied blocker of TASK-003/004/021/068/074 in blocked[]; gates on milestones never release. Class: any kind whose status is mechanically derivable (the state_derivation.rollups vocabulary) is read RAW by every bucket-consumer — gate satisfaction, focus_fallback, lens views, contextState summaries — so derived truth and consumed truth can diverge permanently. The packaged milestone schema additionally cites a 'substrate-derived-state' canon that does not exist in the conventions block.

evidence.reference: isCompleted buckets index.byRefname item's stored status; feeds unsatisfiedGates (:835-836) and therefore blocked/nextActions
milestones[] rollup derived per read from membership edges + member-status buckets, never persisted, never consulted by isCompleted
status description: DERIVED phase-rollup, not authored — authored status rejected by canon (substrate-derived-state — a convention id absent from the conventions block)
live-confirmed probe (P1) + seam map; reached-milestone-phases-complete one-directionality confirmed via context-validate run emitting no milestone issue

gaps.impact: A reached milestone does not release the tasks gated on it — MILE-003 derives reached while TASK-003/004/021/068/074 still derive blocked, so milestone completion has no effect on ready/blocked truth; the currentState payload self-contradicts; the divergence is invisible to context-validate.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Per analysis/2026-07-05-currency-foreclosure-shape.md Class A (the schema_version construction generalized): isCompleted consults the rollup for rollup kinds (config-declared via state_derivation.rollups — closes the self-contradiction by construction within one read), converge-on-write stamps the stored field at the write chokes, a derived-status invariant class detects both divergence directions out-of-band, and a context-reconcile op repairs existing substrates. Determination and decomposition at the derivation-truthfulness feature filing; the dangling substrate-derived-state canon citation is corrected there too.
gaps.closed_by: TASK-084/085/086/087 (VER-072/073/074/075) — the filed resolution's four mechanisms all shipped: gate satisfaction consults the derived rollup for config-declared rollup kinds within the same currentState read (the self-contradiction impossible by construction); the derived-status invariant class detects stored-vs-derived divergence in BOTH directions out-of-band; context-reconcile repairs with a dry-run-exact preview through the validated write path (and converged the live MILE-003 instance); and converge-on-write leaves rollup-kind stored statuses equal to their derivation after every sanctioned rollup-input write (demonstrated through the promoted operator: one phase update left the milestone stored 'reached' on disk). All four verdicts come from ONE shared completeness helper (derivedRollupComplete), so derivation, detector, repair, and convergence cannot disagree. The dangling substrate-derived-state canon citation remains TASK-091's deliverable.

gaps.title: Read surface has no field projection — item-metadata enumeration requires full-item reads the 50KB cap refuses, so substrate-wide currency audits are not cheaply repeatable
gaps.description: No read op projects a subset of item fields: filter-block-items, read-block-page, resolve-items-by-id, and context-lens-view --bin all return whole items. Enumerating one field across a block (e.g. every research item's status + stale_conditions, or every gap's id + status + priority) therefore costs full-item payloads, and on populated blocks the 50KB read cap correctly refuses (observed in the 2026-07-05 currency audit: filter-block-items on framework-gaps status-in refused at 127,873 bytes; on research status-eq refused at 135,562 bytes; read-block-page of 8 research items truncated at 59,257 bytes). The workarounds are per-item read-block-item loops (dozens of invocations) or table-format rendering (drops fields not in the fixed column set and falls back to full JSON on heterogeneous pages). Class: any substrate-wide sweep that needs one or two fields per item — currency audits, staleness-condition review, priority triage, the planned reconcile/staleness machinery's reporting — pays whole-item cost or is refused; the cap and the projection absence jointly make honest bulk enumeration impossible through the sanctioned surface. Over-cap filter queries additionally fail as count-without-ids: the fail-closed envelope preserves total/totalBytes but bounds data to null, so the surface answers how-many-match while refusing which (observed 2026-07-06: filter-block-items framework-gaps description-matches 'substrate' → data:null, total:37, totalBytes:137380, complete:false); the op's overCapDirective names read-block-page / refine-the-predicate — both row-narrowing, neither reaches the match set's ids, and read-block-page's own floor (--limit 1) is bounded by the widest single item. Under-cap queries wanting only ids pay whole-body cost (observed 2026-07-06: conventions match-all → 14 items, 22,541 bytes of full rule bodies for ~400 bytes of wanted ids; --fields rejected as unknown flag). Survey completes the class enumeration: join-blocks, read-block, read-block-dir, and gather-execution-context also return whole item bodies; the only narrow shapes on the surface — context-lens-view's bin→count summary, walk-ancestors/context-walk-descendants id-chains, find-references Edge[] — are hardcoded, not parameterized, and cover only their own axes. Distinct from FGAP-016 (which concerns the cap measuring the pretty form vs compact emission, not projection).

evidence.reference: filter-block-items / read-block-page / resolve-items-by-id declare no field-projection parameter; context-lens-view --bin pages whole items
the audit that hit the refusals; the recommended reconcile/staleness machinery needs cheap enumeration of status + condition fields across blocks

structureForRead bounds data to null on over-cap (line 313) while total/totalBytes survive — the count-without-ids envelope; pageArray (182-193) is the one-implementation precedent a projectFields primitive would sit beside

filter-block-items overCapDirective names read-block-page + refine-the-predicate — row-narrowing only; join-blocks whereField/whereOp/whereValue trio and resolve-items-by-id ids Type.Array(Type.String()) are the replicated-parameter and array-parameter precedents for a fields parameter
the 2026-07-06 investigation: both failure shapes reproduced with envelopes pasted, full read-surface survey table, prior-art triage

gaps.impact: Substrate-wide currency auditing degrades to per-item invocation loops or partial table renders; the exact sweeps the derivation-truthfulness machinery must run (stored-vs-derived status, fired stale_conditions) cannot be performed affordably through the read surface.
gaps.proposed_resolution: A shared projectFields primitive beside pageArray in read-element.ts, applied to item bodies BEFORE structureForRead serializes (truncateHead measures the projected payload — the cap shrinks rather than truncates), surfaced as an optional fields: string[] parameter (Type.Optional(Type.Array(Type.String())), reflecting to --fields) on the item-returning query ops — primary: filter-block-items, read-block-page, context-lens-view --bin; secondary: join-blocks, resolve-items-by-id. id always retained; a named field absent on an item is absent in the projected row; omitted fields = whole-item (additive, backward-compatible). Parameter-shape precedents: resolve-items-by-id ids (array param), join-blocks where* trio (one narrowing set replicated across ops).

gaps.title: release:* bumps versions but never re-promotes — the globally-installed operator CLI silently lags the tree after every release
gaps.description: scripts/release.mjs bumps the lockstep versions, rolls the CHANGELOGs, commits, and tags — but never runs the operator promote step (scripts/promote-cli.mjs), so the globally-installed pi-context binary (a packed copy of the tree at promote time) is left at the pre-release version after every release run. The dogfooding discipline says re-promote after editing/building, but the release script is itself the causal write that moves the version, and nothing at that seam converges the operator. Observed live: immediately after the v0.32.0 release run the global operator reported 0.31.0 against tree 0.32.0 and required a manual promote.

evidence.reference: steps end at commit + tag ('Next steps (human): npm publish … git push …'); no promote invocation
the promote step — packs and installs the tree copy into the global npm prefix; only ever run manually

gaps.impact: Every release leaves the operator one version behind until someone remembers to promote; substrate reads/writes dogfood a stale binary and version-sensitive behavior (new ops, new reason vocabularies, migration chains) diverges silently between the released tree and the operator driving it.
gaps.proposed_resolution: scripts/release.mjs ends by running the promote step after the tag, printing the promoted version, so a release run leaves the operator at the released version by construction. A promote failure at that point is reported loudly but does not un-release (the bump/tag are already committed).
gaps.closed_by: TASK-083 / VER-071 — scripts/release.mjs step 5: after the tag, the release run promotes the operator CLI (npm run promote:cli via the script's run() helper, silent+ignoreError so a string return means success and null means failure) and prints the promoted version, so a release leaves the globally-installed pi-context at the released version by construction. A promote failure does not un-release: the bump/CHANGELOG/commit/tag are already durable; the script reports the release intact, names npm run promote:cli as the manual remedy, reprints the human next steps, and exits non-zero. Both legs runtime-demonstrated (throwaway-prefix convergence to the tree version; unwritable-prefix promote failure). README + CLAUDE.md state the behavior. Merged e1647eef.

gaps.title: A failing message-less assert under the tsx test loader spins node:assert's message reconstruction effectively forever — 958 bare assert occurrences are latent suite-hang triggers
gaps.description: When an assert.ok/assert with NO message argument FAILS in a test file loaded by tsx, node:assert reconstructs the failure expression by re-reading the module source and locating the call (getErrMessage -> getCode -> findColumn -> acorn parseExpressionAt). tsx serves the TRANSFORMED module as a single line whose positions do not map back to the on-disk TypeScript, so the acorn re-parse never converges — the test child process spins at 100% CPU indefinitely (>=49 minutes observed), its event loop starved, and the node test runner's parent waits forever with the log frozen mid-run and no summary. Proven via V8-inspector pause on two independent spinning processes with the stack anchored at the transformed frame (analysis/2026-07-05-converge-hook-hang-rootcause.md; a minimal twin-file repro measured message-present at 0.36s vs message-less unbounded). The repo carries ~958 message-less assert/assert.ok occurrences across test files — each a latent hang trigger on any future failure. Mitigation shipped with the converge-on-write task: every tsx-runner package test script carries --test-timeout=120000, which turns a hang into a bounded, NAMED test failure (measured). Residual class: (a) the messaged-assert sweep so failures report instantly instead of at the deadline; (b) a CI wall-clock with SIGKILL — the runner's SIGTERM at the deadline is absorbed by signal-exit on a starved loop, so an external kill guard is needed for unattended runs.

evidence.reference: reproduction table, V8-inspector stack (assert.ok -> getErrMessage -> getCode -> findColumn -> acorn parseExpressionAt at the single-line transformed frame), twin-file timing measurement, and the --test-timeout behavior measurement

test script now carries --test-timeout=120000 (mitigation; same for the other four tsx-runner packages). Pin re-reviewed 2026-07-08: the file changed by the v0.33.0 lockstep version bump — the test-script mitigation the citation attests is unchanged.

gaps.impact: Any future failing bare assert in any tsx-run test file stalls that package's suite until the 120s deadline (attended) or requires an external kill (unattended CI on a starved loop) — and before the mitigation it hung runs indefinitely and silently, costing a half-hour of wall clock in the observed instance.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Class fix in two legs: (1) messaged-assert sweep — every assert/assert.ok in test files carries a message argument (mechanical, partitionable; a lint rule or grep-gate keeps new bare asserts out); (2) CI hardening — the workflow wraps npm test in an external wall-clock that SIGKILLs on overrun, since the runner's own deadline cannot deliver SIGTERM to a starved event loop. Determination at task time whether a biome/lint rule can enforce leg 1 structurally.

gaps.title: block-pi-context-glue.sh pattern-matches shell metacharacters inside QUOTED argument values as shell syntax — regex alternation in --value is blocked as piping, and every heuristic false-positives on quoted-payload text
gaps.description: .claude/hooks/block-pi-context-glue.sh runs all four block heuristics (pipe/stderr-silence line 25, for/while-do loop line 30, stdout-redirect line 38, echo/$? line 43) and the pi-context invocation recognizer (line 22) as grep -E over the RAW tool_input.command string with no quote-span awareness, so metacharacters that are DATA inside quoted argument values match as shell syntax. Confirmed false-positive matrix (scratchpad hook copy + one live block): | inside a quoted --value (single- and double-quoted, incl. escaped \") blocks as piping; literal 2>/dev/null text in a quoted payload blocks as stderr-silence; prose 'for … do' in a quoted value blocks as a loop; ' > ' inside a quoted JSON payload blocks as redirect; $? in a quoted payload blocks as exit-capture; '; echo ' inside a quoted value satisfies the echo anchor from inside the quotes; and the recognizer fires on a NON-pi-context command (git commit -m) whose quoted message mentions 'pi-context … |'. Non-members confirming the boundary: bare word 'echo' in a value passes (command-position anchor holds); '#' in a payload passes (no comment heuristic). Class: any hook pattern element not lexically confined to inter-token shell syntax matches identical characters occurring as data inside quoted values. Distinct from FGAP-089 (same hooks, sibling class: target-substrate scope-blindness; this gap is command-string quote-blindness — fixing one does not fix the other).

evidence.reference: line 25: (pi-context |bin\.js )[^;&|]*\| — [^;&|]* crosses into the quoted value and \| matches a data pipe; the 2>/dev/null alternative is fully unanchored

lines 30, 38, 43: loop / redirect / $?-capture patterns each matched inside quoted payload text in the probe matrix; the echo alternative's (^|[;&|]) anchor is satisfiable from inside quotes ('; echo ')
line 22: recognizer (^|[;&|]| )pi-context  matches a quoted MENTION of pi-context in an unrelated command, pulling it into the guard

the investigation: live block transcript for filter-block-items --value '"hook|guard"', 15-case FP/TP/CLEAN probe matrix against a scratchpad hook copy, class verdict, prior-art sweep (FGAP-089 sibling-not-duplicate), and a validated one-line quote-span-stripping prototype (all 5 TPs still block, all 9 FPs pass)

gaps.impact: Substrate search via filter-block-items --op matches cannot use regex alternation — the guard blocks the exact narrowing remedy its own block message prescribes — degrading every multi-term substrate query (incl. the mandatory prior-art sweep before any filing) to one term per invocation. Any inline --item/--updates payload whose text discusses shell glue (which gap filings about this discipline necessarily do) trips the redirect/pipe/exit-capture checks. Observed blocking live work at least three times (two prior sessions + this investigation's live repro).
gaps.proposed_resolution: Make the hook quote-aware at the chokepoint, closing the class for all five pattern sites at once: strip quoted spans from the command string with a single left-to-right combined substitution (perl -pe "s/'[^']*'|\"(?:\\\\.|[^\"\\\\])*\"/Q/g") immediately after extracting tool_input.command, then run the existing regexes unchanged against the stripped skeleton. Prototyped on a scratchpad copy: all 5 true-positive glue forms still exit 2 with the correct message, all 3 clean forms and all 9 false-positive forms exit 0; left-to-right combined matching is required (two-pass stripping mishandles interleaved quotes and \" escapes — the escaped-double-quote case stayed blocked under a naive two-pass sed and passed only after the combined-pattern upgrade). Degradation on exotic quoting is fail-closed (over-block, never under-block), proportionate to the cooperative-agent enforcement target. Fallback if under-blocking ever surfaces: tokenize with a real shell lexer and inspect only inter-token operators. Acceptance test = the investigation's probe matrix (every TP row exit 2, every CLEAN and FP row exit 0). Develop against a COPY, never the live guard; relate to FGAP-089 as sibling class on the same artifact. Grounding: analysis/2026-07-06-glue-hook-quoted-value-false-positive.md.
gaps.closed_by: Quote-blindness closed by making the guard quote-aware: a single left-to-right COMBINED perl substitution (block-pi-context-glue.sh line 32) strips each single- and double-quoted span (honoring \" escapes) to a placeholder 'Q' immediately after tool_input.command extraction, BEFORE the recognizer and all four block heuristics — so shell metacharacters that are DATA inside quoted argument values are no longer visible as shell syntax, while inter-token operators outside quotes survive (genuine glue still blocks) and degradation on exotic quoting is fail-closed (over-block, never under-block). Composes with the pipe-evasion fix already in the file (block-1/block-3 redirect-tolerant bridges); the strip runs on a skeleton the existing regexes consume unchanged. Verified against the live deployed guard: the extended harness (.claude/hooks/block-pi-context-glue.test.sh) 28/28 — every pre-existing pipe-evasion case plus the quote-FP rows (single/double-quoted | in --value, literal 2>/dev/null and ' > ' and $? and '; echo ' and prose 'for x do' inside quoted payloads, and a non-pi-context git commit -m mentioning the CLI); raw checks — filter-block-items --value '"hook|guard"' passes (rc=0), the same command with a genuine trailing | head blocks (rc=2). Sibling on the same artifact: FGAP-089 (target-substrate scope-blindness, still open — a distinct class the quote fix does not address). Grounding: analysis/2026-07-06-glue-hook-quoted-value-false-positive.md (landing note appended).

gaps.title: Birth-relations entries afford only the raw direction form — the 14 orientation-ambiguous role-typed relation_types cannot be filed as birth edges, so the filing transition atom is unusable exactly where FGAP-113's write guard demands the role form
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: birth-relations role-typed orientation parity (filing-atom entry shape affords primary/counter)
gaps.description: The relations filing parameter on append-block-item / append-mode upsert-block-item (244e3d43) shape-locks each entry to {relation_type, direction: as_parent|as_child, other, ordinal?} (BirthRelation, ops-registry.ts; coerceBirthRelations refuses any other key set) and appendBirthRelations translates every entry into a bare {parent, child} appendRelationByRef call. orientAppendInput (context-sdk.ts, the FGAP-113 guard) REJECTS a bare append of any relation that declares role_direction and is orientation-ambiguous per relationKindsOverlap (shared kind, '*' wildcard, or undeclared kind set), instructing re-issue with --primary/--counter — an instruction the birth shape cannot express. Affected: 14 of the 17 role-bearing relation_types in the live registry (all six same-kind ordering relations: decision_supersedes_decision, task_depends_on_task, story_depends_on_story, requirement_depends_on_requirement, research_supersedes_research, milestone_precedes_milestone; all wildcard gated-by/derived-from/includes/depends: feature_depends_on_item, story_includes_item, task_gated_by_item, feature_gated_by_item, story_gated_by_item, decision_gated_by_item, item_derived_from_item, decision_derived_from_item); the 3 disjoint-kind role-bearing relations and all 27 role-less relations are unaffected. Class, not one symptom: the birth entry shape affords a strict subset of the standalone append-relation/append-relations porcelain — no role-typed pair, and no preview parity (upsert --dryRun reports would-file for birth entries without running the orientation guard, so preview passes what the live run refuses; append-block-item has no dryRun). Severity interaction: the error-invariant-transition-atom convention names filing-with-relations as the birth-edge atom; for the 14 types the atom cannot express the edge, so an error-severity requires-edge invariant demanding one deadlocks filings on that block (empirically demonstrated: at error severity the bare filing is gate-refused, the atom is orientation-refused, and the two-op sequence's first op is gate-refused), and convention rule 2 (name the atom before raising to error) is unsatisfiable for those types.

evidence.reference: BirthRelation shape (direction-only) + coerceBirthRelations key-set refusal + appendBirthRelations building unconditionally-bare {parent, child} appendRelationByRef calls
relationKindsOverlap overlap definition ('*' or shared kind or undeclared set) + orientAppendInput ambiguous-bare-append reject whose remedy (--primary/--counter) has no birth-shape expression
upsert-block-item dryRun reports a would-file birth-relation count without orientation validation — preview passes an entry the live run refuses (the second subset cell)

read-config --registry relation_types: 44 registered, 17 role-bearing, 14 orientation-ambiguous (computed 2026-07-06); read-config --registry invariants: 13 declared — only decision-shows-derivation (warning) demands an affected type (decision_derived_from_item), no error invariant does

investigation of record: root-cause trace, affected-set computation, library-level reproduction transcripts (refusal + byte-identical restore on an invariant-declaring fixture; standalone primary/counter success; error-severity three-path deadlock demo), prior-art map (FGAP-113 closed origin, uncovered surface), class characterization

gaps.impact: Every birth filing carrying one of the 14 affected relation_types is refused with an unfollowable instruction, forcing the edge into a second standalone op — a non-atomic sequence that survives today only because the sole live invariant demanding an affected type (decision-shows-derivation) is warning severity. The affected set is the gated-by / derived-from / supersedes / depends families — the relation_types most natural as birth edges. Under any error-severity requires-edge invariant demanding one of the 14 (the documented backfill-then-raise trajectory for decision-shows-derivation is the nearest case), filings on that block deadlock: bare filing gate-refused, atom orientation-refused, two-op sequence gate-refused — the exact class the error-invariant-transition-atom convention exists to prevent, and the skill narrative's stated guarantee (the atomic filing form is the path the gate accepts) becomes no-path. The upsert dryRun preview additionally false-passes such entries.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Give the birth entry both orientation forms, single-sourcing guard + mapping in orientAppendInput: each entry carries {relation_type, other, ordinal?} plus EITHER direction: as_parent|as_child (raw, unchanged) OR role: primary|counter (the NEW item holds that role; other holds the complement), mutually exclusive per entry (coerceBirthRelations enforces). appendBirthRelations maps role entries to the role-typed RelationAppendInput (primary → {primary: itemId, counter: other}; counter → {primary: other, counter: itemId}) and delegates unchanged, so the existing orientAppendInput errors apply verbatim (role on a role-less relation → existing no-role_direction throw; direction on an ambiguous role-bearing relation → existing re-issue throw, now followable in-shape). Preview parity: run the orientation mapping/guard over coerced entries under upsert --dryRun (endpoint resolution may stay out; orientation needs only the registry). Regression locks per guard path plus the error-severity deadlock demo passing post-fix via the role form. Docs-surface-sync: both op strings in ops-registry.ts, packages/pi-context/README.md write-pipeline + Block CRUD prose, skill-narrative.md atomic-filing paragraph, SKILL.md regen, CHANGELOG [Unreleased].
gaps.closed_by: TASK-092 (VER-077) — the birth-relations entry shape affords both orientation forms: role: primary|counter maps to the role-typed append input and delegates to the same porcelain, so orientAppendInput's mapping + ambiguity guard apply verbatim and its re-issue instruction is followable in-shape; the direction/role mutual exclusion refuses at coercion before any write; upsert --dryRun runs the same orientation guard over entries (preview refuses what the live run refuses — the second class cell). The error-severity deadlock demonstration passes via the role form, pinned as a test; demonstrated live through the promoted CLI (direction form refused, role form threading an error derivation invariant atomically, stored orientation read back per role_direction). error-invariant-transition-atom rule 2 is satisfiable for all 17 role-bearing relation_types.

gaps.title: Derivation is foreign-blind: every edge-consuming derived surface resolves endpoints against the active index only, so valid foreign endpoints are silently dropped (gates derive ready), defaulted (rollups never complete), or collision-mis-resolved to same-refname local items — while validate certifies the same edges as fine
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: registry-aware endpoint resolution in derived state
gaps.description: The v0.28.0 cross-substrate arc shipped foreign IDENTITY (structured EdgeEndpoint with substrate_id, registry+aliases, resolveRef active/foreign/dangling/unregistered classification, promoteItem lineage) but no derivation surface consumes it. Every derivation/read join computes endpointKey(e) — the BARE refname for a structured foreign endpoint (normalizeEndpoint key: refname ?? oid, substrate_id discarded) — and looks it up in the ACTIVE substrate's byRefname index only. Consequences by surface: (1) gate/dependency satisfaction (currentState incompletePreds/unsatisfiedGates, context-sdk.ts:889-892) filters foreign blockers out via index.byRefname.has() BEFORE the completeness check — the foreign endpoint rides the documented dangling-target-satisfied guard, so a task gated on an OPEN foreign item derives ready/nextActions; (2) derivedRollupComplete (context-sdk.ts:758-759) returns false for any foreign member — a rollup with a foreign member NEVER derives complete even when every member is complete in its home substrate, and context-reconcile/converge-on-write STAMP that false incomplete_status onto the stored item; (3) the status-consistency invariant skips foreign targets (context-sdk.ts:2352-2353 'if (!otherLoc) continue', comment wrongly claims edge-integrity handles it); (4) requires-edge satisfaction never resolves the counter-endpoint (foreign or dangling edges satisfy); (5) joinBlocks edge mode (context-sdk.ts:1253) and roadmap-plan rollups (roadmap-plan.ts:340,345) drop foreign counterparts; (6) walk ops emit foreign nodes as bare refnames (substrate qualifier erased). Refname collision is the aggravator: because the foreign key is the bare refname, a local item with the same refname (the EXPECTED case across catalog-installed sibling substrates) silently substitutes its status for the foreign item's — empirically, a task gated on foreign-open gB derived UNBLOCKED because a local closed gB existed. Meanwhile validateContext's edge-integrity DOES route through resolveRef and classifies foreign endpoints as VALID (no issue), so the substrate certifies edges that its own derivation treats as nonexistent. Zero diagnostics on any derivation path. The 2026-05-31 identity spec designed the resolver wiring for validation (Phase J1, shipped) and foreign-aware status-consistency (§J3: compare-if-readable, warn-if-unreadable — NOT implemented); it contains no design for derivation over foreign endpoints. findReferences (raw Edge[] out) is the only edge-read surface preserving foreignness.

evidence.reference: lines 889-892: incompletePreds/unsatisfiedGates filter blockers through index.byRefname.has() against the ACTIVE index — foreign gate/dep endpoints silently dropped before the completeness check

lines 405-413: normalizeEndpoint — foreign structured endpoint keys on bare refname (refname ?? oid), substrate_id discarded — the collision-aliasing root; endpointKey consumers cannot distinguish foreign from local/dangling

lines 758-759: derivedRollupComplete — unresolvable (foreign) member returns false, rollup never complete; shared by currentState gate satisfaction, the derived-status invariant, and reconcile/converge (index.ts:2704,2760), which WRITE the false derivation to stored status

lines 2352-2353: status-consistency invariant skips foreign targets ('if (!otherLoc) continue'); comment claims edge-integrity handles it, but edge-integrity classifies foreign as valid (no issue) — the drift is checked nowhere. Spec §J3 designed compare-if-readable/warn-if-unreadable for exactly this site; unimplemented

lines 2513-2557: validateContext edge loop routes every endpoint through resolveRef, foreign → no issue — the substrate certifies foreign edges as valid while deriving as if absent; split-brain empirically demonstrated (blocked:[] + nextActions:[tA] + validate clean, with a declared status-consistency invariant, task gated on an OPEN foreign gap via a porcelain-written structured foreign endpoint)

line 1253: joinBlocks edge mode resolves the counter-endpoint via active byRefname only; roadmap-plan.ts:340,345 likewise for phase/task rollup members; context.ts:1586-1638 walks emit bare refnames (foreign qualifier erased)

investigation of record: mechanism trace, two-substrate empirical reproduction (fixture + pasted outputs, incl. the refname-collision mis-resolution and the both-members-complete milestone deriving planned), per-surface class inventory, prior-art sweep, resolution shapes

gaps.impact: Cross-substrate edges — the entire point of the v0.28.0 identity arc — carry no derived consequence anywhere: a foreign gate silently releases (false-ready enters nextActions and is composed into agent contexts), a foreign rollup member permanently pins its container incomplete AND reconcile/converge durably stamp that wrong status, declared invariants silently skip foreign targets, and a refname collision (expected across catalog-installed substrates) makes derivation read the WRONG substrate's status as the gate truth. validate certifies all of it clean, so no audit surfaces the divergence. Downstream two-substrate projects hit this on first real use; concurrent operational peer substrates (per-pillar topologies) are foreclosed until fixed.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Route every edge-consuming derivation join through registry-aware endpoint resolution (resolveRef / a resolveEndpointLoc(e) shim with the existing per-pass foreignCache) instead of endpointKey+active-byRefname: gate/dep predicates, derivedRollupComplete (the one seam converging currentState, the derived-status invariant, and reconcile/converge), status-consistency (implementing spec §J3's compare-if-readable/warn-if-unreadable), joinBlocks, roadmap rollups, walks (qualify foreign keys in output). In every variant: eliminate silent dropping (unresolvable-foreign degrades LOUDLY), split the dangling-vs-foreign conflation in the gate guard, and kill refname-collision aliasing (foreign keys must never collide onto local items). The foreign-resolution SEMANTICS — live cross-substrate status read vs derive-as-blocked-until-resolved vs flagged-exclusion — is a genuine design choice (freshness/cost/offline-substrate trade-offs) and lands as a DECISION first; requires-edge foreign-satisfaction is examined and decided in the same decision. Addressed by FEAT-012 (cross-substrate consumption); relates to FGAP-061 (same deriver, orthogonal kind-coverage axis) and FGAP-002 (the identity/provenance layer this consumption layer completes); sibling instance in the rendering stack recorded as R-0009 finding D1 (template resolve() is single-substrate).

gaps.title: Authoring-time shape projections flatten nested item shapes — --show-schema and the samples-catalog itemProperties render object/array<object> fields as bare types, and every documented pre-step points at the names-only required read, so the refused write is the de-facto nested-shape discovery mechanism
gaps.description: Two independent one-level projections hide nested item contracts: (1) the --show-schema render (pi-context-cli cli.ts:1028-1041) types each field as fschema.type | $ref | 'enum' | 'object' — a nested object prints as bare 'object' (no keys), an array<object> as 'array' (no items keys, no items.required); (2) schemaInfoFromPath (pi-context context-sdk.ts:209-264, comment 'one level deep' at 232) emits SchemaProperty {name,type,required,description?,enum?} with no nested-children field, and samples-catalog.ts:175 projects it as read-samples-catalog shape.itemProperties — research.grounding surfaces as {type:'object'} with no keys, citations as 'array' with no items shape. Guidance compounds it: the CLAUDE.md canonical filing pre-step reads properties.<key>.items.required (names only), read-schema's sole example (ops-registry.ts:1473) addresses the same .required path, and SKILL.md's read-schema description says to address ONE property 'instead of reading the whole schema' — while the sufficient one-call read (read-schema --path properties.<key>.items; 6,311 bytes for research, every catalog schema ≤10,251 bytes, all under the 50KB cap) is named nowhere. samples/blocks/*.json ship empty arrays — no exemplar items. 8 of 18 catalog schemas carry nested shapes invisible to every projection: context-contracts.bundle_relation_types, decisions.options_considered+references, framework-gaps.evidence, layer-plans.layers+migration_phases, phase.success_criteria+specs, research.grounding+citations, verification.criteria_results, work-orders.scope+real_check_criteria. Observed instance (2026-07-06): a research filing authored after the documented pre-step passed grounding as a string and citations as [{path,note}]; the append was refused whole with per-field diagnostics; two follow-up per-field read-schema calls recovered the shapes. The refusal is FGAP-022's filed impact ('raising write-time validation failures') recurring one nesting level below the preview it shipped.

evidence.reference: lines 1028-1041: --show-schema field loop — type = fschema.type | $ref | 'enum' | 'object'; no expansion of nested properties or items.required

lines 209-264: schemaInfoFromPath extracts item properties one level deep (comment line 232); SchemaProperty (179-184) has no nested-shape field; consumed by samples-catalog.ts:175 for read-samples-catalog shape.itemProperties

line 1473: read-schema's sole example addresses properties.gaps.items.required — the names-only read; the CLAUDE.md canonical filing pre-step and the SKILL.md read-schema description steer the same way
starter blocks ship empty arrays — no exemplar item anywhere on the authoring surface

investigation of record: affordance inventory with observed byte sizes, 8/18 nested-shape schema enumeration, the refused-write instance, prior-art sweep (not tracked; FGAP-022 origin, FGAP-117/072/089 adjacent-not-covering)

gaps.impact: An author on any of the 8 nested-shape block kinds who follows the documented pre-step or either purpose-built projection authors nested fields blind; first contact with the nested contract is the refused write. The affected fields sit on the every-filing path: framework-gaps.evidence (every gap), verification.criteria_results (every task closure), decisions.options_considered/references (every decision).
gaps.proposed_resolution: Expand nested shapes at both projection sites, one recursion level (covers the entire current catalog — no nested field nests further): the --show-schema render prints an object field's nested keys+types and an array<object> field's item keys/types + items.required; SchemaProperty gains an optional children field populated by schemaInfoFromPath and rendered through read-samples-catalog shape.itemProperties. Correct the guidance to the sufficient one-call read (read-schema --path properties.<key>.items): the CLAUDE.md canonical filing pre-step, the read-schema example in ops-registry.ts, skill-narrative.md, SKILL regen — docs-surface-sync. Additive; in-pi op behavior frozen except the read-schema example string.

gaps.title: In-process jit dispatch cannot execute tools — executeAgent is a single-turn completion primitive, so the grant machinery clamps a capability that does not exist
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: executable tool binding in jit dispatch
gaps.description: executeAgent (packages/pi-jit-agents/src/jit-runtime.ts:432-738) makes one completeFn call and extracts output; it never binds tool implementations. compiled.tools is consumed at exactly one point — the DEC-0047 grant-subset clamp (computeGrantViolation, :488) — gating NAMES, materializing nothing; the only tool ever passed to the LLM is the phantom output-schema tool (:566-570), never executed by design; DispatchContext (types.ts:195-234) has no channel for tool implementations. Empirically demonstrated (instrumented completeFn against built dist with the WO-001 spec): grant ['write','bash'] passes the clamp and is discarded — toolsPassedToLLM: UNDEFINED. The primitive fits its original consumers (monitor classifiers, structured-output workflow steps — output, never action); the work-order loop is the first consumer dispatching an agent whose job is to ACT, and reused the act-less primitive. Class: every in-process jit dispatch (call-agent, run-work-order-loop, future consumers reading .agent.yaml tools: as capability); real tool execution exists only in a pi subprocess. Structural sibling: FGAP-008 (closed — a contract lacking the channel its consumers assume). Severity within the surface: CRITICAL — every honest work-order must fail; the surface is currently unexercised (zero live WOs), hence P2 not P1.

evidence.reference: 432-738 executeAgent: single completeFn call (:572); context.tools populated only by the phantom output-schema tool (:566-570); grant clamp consumes compiled.tools at :488 and nothing else does
195-234 DispatchContext — no tool-implementation channel; 188-189 CompiledAgent.tools documented as grant vocabulary
Break 2: instrumented reproduction (SPEC tools ['write','bash'] → toolsPassedToLLM UNDEFINED), class inventory, why-it-is-shaped-this-way archaeology

gaps.impact: The work-order loop's core promise is void: a dispatched target agent cannot produce any side-effect, so iteration 0 can never pass a real check requiring one — every real work-order fails or aborts. call-agent consumers inherit the same ceiling. The deterministic real-check surfaced this as a clean failure rather than a false pass.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Decided under FEAT-014's dispatch-architecture decision: (a) dispatch acting agents as pi subprocesses (tools exist there; executeAgent stays the classify/structured-output primitive), (b) grow executeAgent a bound tool-execution loop, or (c) re-scope work-orders to output-only. Whichever is decided, the grant machinery must clamp a capability that actually exists on the dispatch path, and the WO-001 probe recipe becomes the regression pin.
gaps.closed_by: Fixed on main (merge 8e2e764e): the work-order loop's dispatchTargetAgent now spawns a pi subprocess (pi --mode json -p <task> --tools <composedGrant> --model <spec>) — the pi-workflows production mechanism — instead of the tool-less in-process executeAgent primitive; the composed capability clamp becomes the subprocess --tools allowlist and now actually reaches execution. New subprocess-dispatch.ts (runPiSubprocess + testable buildDispatchArgs) with timeout/SIGTERM-SIGKILL/cancellation/long-prompt-tmpfile/bounded-stdout; suite 100/100. Verified end to end and re-run independently by the orchestrator with the artifact deleted first: runWorkOrderLoop against a fixture whose agent must write a file produced out.txt='FGAP-124-PROVEN', the deterministic runtime-demo real-check passed, final_status=completed. Operator rebuilt + promoted. Residual (separately tracked, not part of this defect): FGAP-125 (loop still ignores input_contract/context_blocks/output_contract/scope.operations), FGAP-127 (agents tier unseeded), FGAP-126 (non-interactive gate path).

gaps.title: Work-order schema is a v1 contract whose engine implements a demo subset — four required fields never consumed, and the catalog schema asserts a scope clamp that does not run
gaps.description: The work-orders schema requires input_contract, context_blocks, output_contract, and scope; the loop originally consumed none of their semantics. Two are now honored (fixed): the dispatch input is validated against input_contract via the canonical pi-context AJV validator before compile/spawn (validateWorkOrderInput), and the capability grant is clamped to wo.scope.operations (clampToScope intersects the composed grant, so a caller grant of [write,bash] under scope.operations [write] dispatches with --tools write). Two remain unconsumed: context_blocks is never injected into the dispatched agent, and output_contract is never validated against the agent's returned output — both have zero references in pi-agent-dispatch. The catalog schema's scope description historically asserted a clamp that did not run; the clamp now runs, so that assertion is satisfied for scope, while the schema still declares context_blocks/output_contract semantics the engine does not implement. Class: schema-contract ahead of engine (sibling: ContextBlockRef, pi-jit-agents types.ts — documents its deferral). Direction settled by git dates: the schema landed one day ahead of the loop's demo-subset engine.

evidence.reference: dispatchTargetAgent: input validated against wo.input_contract (validateWorkOrderInput) before compile/spawn; the composed grant clamped to wo.scope.operations (clampToScope) becomes the subprocess --tools allowlist — both FIXED. context_blocks and output_contract still have zero consumption in this file.
required nine fields; still declares context_blocks + output_contract semantics the engine does not implement (scope clamp now satisfied)
Break 3: per-field verification with quotes, git-date archaeology, the WO-001 scope demonstration

gaps.impact: Filed work-orders carry dead contract fields authors reasonably rely on: inputs are never validated, context never injected, outputs never checked, and a narrower wo.scope.operations does not narrow the grant. Any substrate installing the catalog schema inherits contract text promising enforcement that does not exist.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Under FEAT-014, the two remaining fields: inject wo.context_blocks into the dispatched pi subprocess (resolve the referenced blocks and pass them into the agent's context across the process boundary) and validate the agent's returned output against wo.output_contract post-return (the subprocess must emit structured output the loop can check) — or amend the schema to stop declaring semantics the engine will not implement, dual-surface, with per-field regression pins. The scope.operations clamp and input_contract validation are already implemented with unit pins (clampToScope, validateWorkOrderInput).

gaps.title: The eight pi-only auth-gated tools have no non-interactive invocation path, and the gate refuses the ceremony while passing the composite that embeds its effect
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: non-interactive completion path for gated ceremonies
gaps.description: The auth gate refuses unconditionally when ctx.hasUI === false (auth-gate.ts:156-161) — deliberate, documented ('closes the JSON-mode / workflow-subprocess bypass', :22-25). The gap is compositional, two facets: (1) the 13 pi-context auth-gated ops are also reflected on the pi-context CLI with --yes pre-authorization, but the 8 pi-only gated tools (author-agent-spec, author-tool-grant, commit-attested, workflow-execute/resume/init, monitors-control/rules) exist only as pi tools — zero non-interactive route; hand-writing the artifact (what the WO-001 probe did for the agent spec) is the only workaround and bypasses the sanctioned ceremony entirely. (2) Gate-coverage inconsistency: the gate operates on tool NAMES; run-work-order-loop is ungated and invokes the commit path as a library call (work-order-loop.ts:171 _internals.attestedCommit), not via the gated commit-attested tool — a passing pi -p run would produce an attested commit with no operator confirm while the less consequential spec authoring is refused. Class: instance of the FGAP-068/069 hasUI=false refusal class (tracked-closed for the schema-conflict instance; resolution vocabulary caller-as-reconciler); this instance and the coverage inconsistency are untracked.

evidence.reference: :67 dispatchGatedTools; :91-96 AUTH_REQUIRED_TOOLS aggregation; :156-161 unconditional hasUI=false refusal; :22-25 the documented intent
attestedCommit invoked as a library call inside the ungated run-work-order-loop — the gated commit-attested tool's effect without its gate
Break 1: the pi -p refusal reproduction, the 13-vs-8 tool inventory, the coverage-inconsistency analysis

gaps.impact: Any harness or workflow driving pi non-interactively cannot complete the eight gated ceremonies at all — forcing hand-written artifacts that skip validation and attestation — while a composite tool can produce a gated ceremony's effect (an attested commit) with no confirm, inverting the gate's risk ordering.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Under FEAT-014: apply the FGAP-068 caller-as-reconciler shape (the interactive orchestrator authors; non-interactive steps never need the gated ceremony) or give the pi-only gated tools an explicit pre-authorization channel equivalent to the CLI --yes, as a decision; close the coverage inconsistency by gating the composite (run-work-order-loop) or routing its commit through the gated tool path.

gaps.title: No ceremony seeds the agents tier the jit loader resolves from — a fresh substrate cannot produce a resolvable target_agent from the loop's own operating context
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: agents-tier seeding in the install ceremony
gaps.description: The jit agent loader's project tier is <contextDir>/agents/ (pi-jit-agents agent-spec.ts:219-243; ~/.pi/agent/agents/ second; builtinDir third only when the caller supplies one). The work-order loop and call-agent construct the loader with cwd only (work-order-loop.ts:116, call-agent-tool.ts:73) — no builtinDir — so the 30+ packaged specs in pi-workflows/agents/ and pi-behavior-monitors/agents/ (resolved by those packages' own loaders) are unreachable for work-order dispatch. Nothing creates or seeds <contextDir>/agents/: context-init/accept-all/install materialize config, schemas, and starter blocks only; no agents concept exists in the install ceremony or samples catalog. The only in-band materializer is author-agent-spec (mkdirs on first authoring, author-agent-spec-tool.ts:82-83) — which is interactively gated (FGAP-126), so on a fresh substrate driven from pi -p the loop's precondition is unproducible. Class: the install ceremony seeds a subset of the surfaces the runtime resolves from (schemas and blocks seeded; agents not; templates similar but package-defaulted).

evidence.reference: 219-243 three-tier search; the builtin tier participates only when the consumer supplies ctx.builtinDir (unchanged, by design — the library never hardcodes a consumer dir); the docblock's stale '.project/agents' tier wording is aligned to the contextDir-resolved reality on fix/fgap-127-bundled-agents-tier

dispatchTargetAgent now constructs the loader via dispatchLoadContext(cwd) (src/dispatch-loader.ts), supplying pi-workflows' bundled agents/ as builtinDir — call-agent-tool.ts rewired identically; the packaged specs are on the dispatch search path (fix on fix/fgap-127-bundled-agents-tier, verification pending)
Break 4: ceremony survey (install copies installed_schemas/installed_blocks only), AgentNotFoundError reproduction, compounding with the gated author tool

gaps.impact: run-work-order-loop on a fresh substrate throws AgentNotFoundError for every target_agent; combined with FGAP-126, the loop's precondition cannot be produced in the loop's own operating context — hand-writing spec files into an unseeded directory is the only path.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Under FEAT-014, per the dispatch-architecture decision: seed the agents tier in the install ceremony (an installed_agents[] manifest beside schemas/blocks) and/or wire a bundled builtinDir into the dispatch loaders; align the loader docblock's stale tier wording.
gaps.closed_by: TASK-103 (VER-081) wired pi-workflows' bundled agents/ as the dispatch loaders' builtin tier via dispatchLoadContext consuming the new @davidorex/pi-workflows/bundled-dirs subpath export — on a fresh substrate whose contextDir/agents is empty, target agents resolve from the 26 packaged specs (runtime-demonstrated via built dist: investigator resolved; local same-named spec still wins; unknown name throws with three tier paths). The loader docblocks' stale .project/agents wording was aligned in the same arc. This takes the proposed_resolution's bundled-builtinDir disjunct; the OTHER disjunct — seeding the agents tier in the install ceremony (an installed_agents[] manifest) — was NOT built and remains an open FEAT-014 design option, deliberately not required for this gap's headline defect (a fresh substrate CAN now produce a resolvable target_agent from the loop's operating context). Compile/model dispatchability beyond resolution was tracked and closed separately as FGAP-129/FGAP-130 (TASK-104, VER-082).

gaps.title: The work-order loop records a no-UI ctx.ui.confirm default as 'aborted-by-human', and its retry path is structurally dead non-interactively
gaps.description: On real-check failure before the last iteration the loop's human-OK gate now checks ctx.hasUI FIRST: in a non-interactive (pi -p) context (ctx.hasUI === false) it does NOT call ctx.ui.confirm and records the distinct final_status 'aborted-non-interactive' instead of mislabeling the noOpUIContext confirm-false default as 'aborted-by-human' — the semantic-mislabel defect is FIXED (mirrors the auth-gate's hasUI-first pattern). The interactive path (a human answering confirm false) still reports 'aborted-by-human', unchanged. Two things remain: (1) there is still no way to opt into non-interactive bounded retry — every non-interactive run is single-shot abort-on-first-failure (fail-SAFE, but not a chosen/opt-in policy); (2) the broader ui.confirm call-site class is unswept — any other ctx.ui.confirm call site that does not pre-check hasUI would treat the no-UI default as an operator decline. Class: FGAP-068 interactivity-dependence in a non-interactively-drivable surface.

evidence.reference: the human-OK gate checks ctx.hasUI === false before ctx.ui.confirm and records aborted-non-interactive (mislabel FIXED); the retry path is still single-shot non-interactively (no opt-in bounded-retry policy)
Break 5: noOpUIContext confirm-false mechanics, the two-defects split, fail-safe-but-dishonest analysis, the ui.confirm call-site sub-class

gaps.impact: The loop's durable verdict lies about the actor (harness inputs must not), and bounded retry — the loop's FEAT-006 governance feature — cannot run in the non-interactive context the harness uses; every such run is single-shot.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Under FEAT-014, the remainder: add an explicit work-order retry policy (on_fail: retry|abort — the sort of field the v1 contract implies, tying to FGAP-125) so non-interactive runs can opt into bounded retry; sweep ui.confirm consumers across the packages for the same no-pre-check pattern and apply the hasUI-first guard where needed. The semantic mislabel (hasUI pre-check + aborted-non-interactive status) is already implemented with a unit pin.

gaps.title: Template-resolution divergence between the two spec pipelines — jit parse absolutizes template refs, so every bundled spec is uncompilable on the dispatch path
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: loader-tier template resolution on the dispatch path
gaps.description: pi-jit-agents parseAgentYaml resolves every relative template ref specDir-relative to an ABSOLUTE path (resolveSpecPath, agent-spec.ts:41-46, applied :191/:193), and renderTemplateFile bypasses the env loader for absolute paths (template.ts:116-126, readFileSync :121-123). The 26 bundled specs in pi-workflows/agents/ all carry template: refs whose files live under pi-jit-agents/templates/ per-agent subdirs (DEC-0049 relocation), not beside the specs — so on the dispatch path (call-agent, work-order loop) loadAgent succeeds and compileAgent throws ENOENT for every bundled spec. The working pi-workflows executor proves the correct shape: its parser keeps refs as loader-resolved NAMES (pi-workflows/src/agent-spec.ts:123-125) through a three-tier env whose builtin tier is bundledTemplateDir() (pi-workflows/src/template.ts:26,29,36) and whose renderTemplateFile has no absolute bypass (:94-100). Compounding: both dispatch sites build createTemplateEnv({ cwd }) with NO builtinDir (call-agent-tool.ts:79, work-order-loop.ts:167), and three analyzer system-templates begin {% extends "analyzers/base-analyzer.md" %} (templates/analyzers/quality.md:1, structure.md:1, patterns.md:1) — loader-resolved even from an absolute entry, so the bundled tier is required in the env regardless. Sibling instance: phase-author.agent.yaml:17 carries a malformed 'templates/' path prefix broken on BOTH pipelines. Class: any consumer of the jit parse + dispatch env combination inherits the failure for any spec whose templates are loader-tier resources; no test on any path compiles a bundled spec (dispatch-loader.test.ts stops at loadAgent; call-agent-tool.test.ts uses inline prompts; pi-workflows agent-spec.test.ts:282-368 is parse-only).

evidence.reference: Class A: root cause, shape, ENOENT reproduction (fresh substrate, investigator), class statement, per-anchor re-verified evidence, prior-art and test-gap survey

resolveSpecPath now absolutizes a relative ref only when it exists beside the spec OR — for bundled-tier specs only (siblingProbe threaded from createAgentLoader's builtin-tier match) — at the package-root sibling location, otherwise preserving it as a loader-resolvable name (TASK-104 fix in flight on fix/fgap-127-bundled-agents-tier; previously unconditional absolutization — the defect this gap tracks; the tier-scoped sibling probe closes the outputSchema member and forecloses the local/user-tier shadowing vector probe 3 surfaced); applied to system/task templates and outputSchema

:116-126 renderTemplateFile absolute-path bypass (readFileSync :121-123); createTemplateEnv tiers :60-81 — builtin tier only when ctx.builtinDir supplied; bundledTemplateDir helper :39-42, now wired at both dispatch sites by the TASK-104 fix in flight

gaps.impact: The FGAP-127 loader fix delivers name-resolution only: every one of the 26 bundled specs fails at compile on both dispatch paths, so bundled-agent dispatch remains impossible end to end; the dispatch template env additionally cannot resolve {% extends %} chains even for local specs.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Converge the dispatch path onto the proven executor mechanism (user-approved plan, 2026-07-08): resolveSpecPath absolutizes a relative ref ONLY when the file exists beside the spec, otherwise returns it unchanged as a loader-resolvable name; both dispatch sites pass builtinDir: bundledTemplateDir() to createTemplateEnv; fix the phase-author.agent.yaml ref; end-to-end compile tests for a bundled spec and an extends-bearing analyzer spec.
gaps.closed_by: TASK-104 (VER-082) converged dispatch template resolution onto the executor's proven mechanism: resolveSpecPath is existence-gated (a relative ref absolutizes only when found beside the spec or — bundled-tier specs only, siblingProbe threaded from createAgentLoader's builtin-tier match — at the package-root sibling location; otherwise it survives as a loader-resolvable name), and both dispatch entry points build their template env with builtinDir: bundledTemplateDir(). Runtime-demonstrated via built dist on a fresh substrate: investigator compiled end-to-end, quality-analyzer compiled through its {% extends %} chain, and all schema-bearing demo specs produced absolute readable parseable outputSchema paths at the exact prior ENOENT point. The sibling instance (phase-author's malformed refs) was repointed in-package. The local/user-tier shadowing vector the tier-scoping forecloses is pinned by real-collision negative cells. Residual observation (probe 4, NIT): phase-author's agent-level schema and the workflows' block:phase step schema are maintained independently and could drift — recorded here, not a defect today (semantic match verified).

gaps.title: Dispatch model-resolution divergence — bundled specs are model-less by design and both dispatch sites hard-throw, with no model-config fallback
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: model-config precedence on the dispatch path
gaps.description: Zero of the 26 bundled specs declare model: — by design ('model comes from .project/model-config.json, not agent spec', pi-workflows/src/agent-spec.test.ts:291). The workflow executor resolves models as step.model ?? agentSpec.model ?? modelConfig.by_role[role] ?? modelConfig.default and OMITS --models when nothing resolves so pi's own default applies (pi-workflows/src/dispatch.ts:76-85; modelConfig read from the substrate model-config block, workflow-executor.ts:765-772). The dispatch path has none of this: call-agent-tool.ts:83-86 and work-order-loop.ts:174-177 hard-throw on a model-less spec, and pi-jit-agents has no fallback (compile.ts:574 passes spec.model verbatim). Class: even after the template class (FGAP-129) is fixed, every model-less spec — the entire bundled set — remains undispatchable on both dispatch paths.

evidence.reference: Class B: root cause, shape, the production precedence facts, per-anchor re-verified evidence

model resolution now compiled.model ?? spec.model ?? resolveDispatchModel(cwd, spec) with the residual throw naming the model-config block (TASK-104 fix in flight; previously an unconditional hard-throw on any model-less spec — the defect this gap tracks; work-order-loop.ts resolves identically and omits the subprocess --model when null)
:76-85 the proven precedence incl. --models omission when nothing resolves; modelConfig sourced from the model-config block (workflow-executor.ts:765-772)

gaps.impact: Bundled-agent dispatch fails 100% of the time on a model axis independent of template resolution; the dispatch surface contradicts the spec-authoring convention that models live in model-config, forcing spec forks or hand-edits.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Per the derived dispatch model-policy decision (user-approved plan, 2026-07-08): a resolveDispatchModel(cwd, spec) helper implementing spec.model ?? model-config.by_role[spec.role] ?? model-config.default (absent block = null fall-through, mirroring the executor); work-order subprocess dispatch omits --model when null (pi defaults inside the subprocess); in-process call-agent keeps the throw when nothing resolves, with the error naming the model-config block as the remedy; precedence unit cells.
gaps.closed_by: TASK-104 (VER-082) implemented DEC-0023's derived precedence in resolveDispatchModel (spec.model, then the substrate model-config block's by_role[spec.role], then its default, else null; absent block = null fall-through mirroring the workflow executor's own try/catch handling): call-agent keeps an informed throw naming the model-config block as the remedy when nothing resolves (the in-process mechanism's floor — executeAgent requires a concrete model), and the work-order subprocess path omits --model when null so pi resolves its own default inside the subprocess (byte-mirroring the executor's --models omission). Precedence unit-pinned (spec wins / by_role / default / absent-block-null / no-match-null); --model presence/omission runtime-demonstrated via built dist; buildDispatchArgs' model made optional preserving its testable export shape.

gaps.title: pi-workflows docs advertise ~30 ${{ }} expression filters; the registry defines 10 — the 20 phantom names throw at runtime, latent because no bundled spec uses one
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: single-source-of-truth filter-surface documentation (generated from FILTER_NAMES, not hand-authored)
gaps.description: expression.ts:10-29 registers exactly 10 ${{ }} filters (duration, currency, json, length, keys, filter, last, first, slugify, shell); FILTER_NAMES = Object.keys(FILTERS) (:32) is re-exported as the SDK filterNames() and consumed by the filter-names validation check. README.md:95-104, SKILL.md:286-288, and skill-narrative.md:40-42 advertise a ~30-name list explicitly in the ${{ }} expression context; 20 of those names (upper, lower, trim, default, join, split, replace, includes, map, sum, min, max, sort, unique, flatten, zip, group_by, count_by, chunk, pick, omit, entries, from_entries, merge, values, not, and, or) are registered in neither the expression layer nor the stock-Nunjucks template layer (template.ts:26-45 registers zero custom filters). An unrecognized filter throws at runtime (expression.ts:110-114); authoring-time validation only warns (workflow-sdk.ts:558-568). No bundled spec uses a phantom name, so the trap is latent — it bites an author who writes a new spec trusting the docs. Class: documentation-ahead-of-implementation; the prose lists bypass the SDK's registry-derived single source of truth.

evidence.reference: Claim 1 evaluation: registered-vs-advertised diff, phantom-name derivation, runtime-throw plus latency proof, class characterization
10-entry FILTERS map; FILTER_NAMES = Object.keys(FILTERS); unknown-filter throw
stock Nunjucks env registers zero custom filters — the phantom names are not mislabeled real Nunjucks filters
advertised ~30-name filter list attached to the ${{ }} expression context
SKILL source carrying the same list; SKILL.md:286-288 is generated from it

gaps.impact: An author who writes a new spec using any of the 20 documented-but-unregistered filters gets a runtime ExpressionError; authoring validation only warns, so the failure surfaces at execution, not at validate time. Three published doc surfaces (README, SKILL.md, skill-narrative.md) misrepresent the filter capability. No shipped workflow is broken.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Derive the documented filter list from filterNames() rather than hand-authoring it, so the three doc surfaces cannot drift from the registry; reconcile the current lists to the 10 registered names — add the four omitted registered names (duration, currency, slugify, shell), remove the 20 phantom names.

gaps.title: 8 of 15 bundled pi-workflows specs are invalid under validateWorkflow; the invalidator is error-level input-contract/template drift, not the missing block schemas (which only warn) — bundled specs have no propagation path from their evolving targets
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: lockstep propagation from evolving targets (catalog vocabulary, agent input contracts, block-schema shapes) to bundled workflow specs
gaps.description: A real validateWorkflow run over all 15 bundled specs (cwd = repo root, active .context) yields 8 invalid / 7 warnings-only. Invalid: analyze-existing-project (4 errors), create-phase (1), do-gap (5), fix-audit (3), gap-to-phase (1), init-new-project (1), pausable-analysis (2), resumable-analysis (2). Warnings-only (0 errors): create-handoff, execute-task, parallel-analysis, parallel-explicit, plan-from-requirements, self-implement, typed-analysis. Missing block schemas (project, architecture, handoff, inventory — absent from installed_blocks) are downgraded to WARNINGS (workflow-sdk.ts:483-492, rationale 'project may not be initialized yet') and do NOT drive invalid status. The invalid verdicts are error-level: inputSchema-required (workflow-sdk.ts:597-614; e.g. analyze-existing-project step infer-project missing required input 'analysis' across 3 agents), template-alignment (workflow-sdk.ts:651-652; e.g. create-phase template references 'gaps' not declared in the step input), and stale schema shape (do-gap step implement: template references spec.intent/spec.tasks/spec.context_needed, fields absent from the current schema whose fields are name, description, files, depends_on, acceptance_criteria, parallel_safe, estimated_complexity). Three manifestation classes: (a) catalog/substrate-vocabulary drift (warning-level) — specs reference block kinds this install's later catalog dropped; (b) stale schema shape (error, do-gap) — spec written against a shape that changed; (c) input-contract drift (error, dominant error source) — agent inputSchema.required keys and template variable references the workflow step no longer provides. Common root: bundled specs carry no propagation path from their evolving targets, so target evolution silently invalidates them.

evidence.reference: Claim 2 evaluation: per-spec status table (8 invalid / 7 warnings), the block-schema-only-warns refinement, the three class characterizations, prior-art sweep
block: schema ref downgraded to warning (:483-492); inputSchema-required error check (:597-614); template-alignment error check (:651-652)
do-gap step-implement template references spec.intent / spec.tasks / spec.context_needed — fields absent from the current spec schema shape

gaps.impact: Over half the bundled workflow catalog fails authoring validation on this substrate; a user validating a shipped spec sees invalid status. Class (a) additionally means analysis/init/handoff/phase specs reference block kinds this install cannot materialize.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Establish a lockstep check that fails when a bundled spec's inputSchema-required keys, template variable references, or block: targets diverge from the current agent specs / block schemas / installed catalog; reconcile the 8 invalid specs to the current contracts (do-gap's spec.intent/tasks/context_needed to the current schema fields; the per-spec input-contract/template mismatches). Relate the catalog-vocabulary facet (a) to FGAP-065 as the pi-context-side instance of the same no-propagation-path family.
